title: Oscar Odds Analysis: Best Adapted Screenplay
text: Winner: Paul Thomas Anderson's One Battle After Another won the Best Adapted Screenplay award at the 98th Academy Awards, confirming market predictions. Pre-Ceremony Odds: One Battle After Another at 96%, Hamnet at 4%, and Train Dreams at 1%. The prediction market showed overwhelming confidence in Anderson's victory. His adaptation of Thomas Pynchon's Vineland had already secured major wins at the Golden Globes, Critics' Choice Awards, and Writers Guild Awards heading into the ceremony. Anderson's script received strong reviews, with the film maintaining a 94% Rotten Tomatoes rating. Critics praised the screenplay's ability to strike a near-impossible balance between characters while satirizing contemporary political movements. Despite 14 prior Oscar nominations, this marked Anderson's first Academy Award win, ending a notable drought in his recognition at the Oscars.
